14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...

32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()

56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()

33	from sglang.srt.function_call.trinity_detector import TrinityDetector
34	from sglang.srt.function_call.utils import get_json_schema_constraint

39	class FunctionCallParser:
40	    """
41	    Parser for function/tool calls in model outputs.
42	
43	    This class handles both streaming and non-streaming parsing of function calls using a detector.
44	    In streaming scenarios, each time new_text is received, it calls detector.parse_streaming_increment
45	    and returns the resulting normal_text and calls to the upper layer (or SSE).
46	    """
47	
48	    ToolCallParserEnum: Dict[str, Type[BaseFormatDetector]] = {
49	        "deepseekv3": DeepSeekV3Detector,
50	        "deepseekv31": DeepSeekV31Detector,
51	        "deepseekv32": DeepSeekV32Detector,
52	        "glm": Glm4MoeDetector,
53	        "glm45": Glm4MoeDetector,
54	        "glm47": Glm47MoeDetector,
55	        "gpt-oss": GptOssDetector,
56	        "kimi_k2": KimiK2Detector,
57	        "lfm2": Lfm2Detector,
58	        "llama3": Llama32Detector,
59	        "mimo": MiMoDetector,
60	        "mistral": MistralDetector,
61	        "pythonic": PythonicDetector,
62	        "qwen": Qwen25Detector,
63	        "qwen25": Qwen25Detector,
64	        "qwen3_coder": Qwen3CoderDetector,
65	        "step3": Step3Detector,
66	        "step3p5": Qwen3CoderDetector,
67	        "minimax-m2": MinimaxM2Detector,
68	        "trinity": TrinityDetector,
69	        "interns1": InternlmDetector,
70	        "hermes": HermesDetector,
71	        "gigachat3": GigaChat3Detector,
72	    }
73	
74	    def __init__(self, tools: List[Tool], tool_call_parser: str):
75	        detector_class = self.ToolCallParserEnum.get(tool_call_parser)
76	        if detector_class:
77	            detector = detector_class()
78	        else:
79	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported tool_call_parser: {tool_call_parser}")
80	
81	        self.detector = detector
82	        self.tools = tools
83	        self.tool_strict_level = envs.SGLANG_TOOL_STRICT_LEVEL.get()
84	
85	    def has_tool_call(self, text: str) -> bool:
86	        """
87	        Check if the given text contains a tool call in the format supported by this parser.
88	        This delegates to the detector's implementation.
89	
90	        Args:
91	            text: The text to check for tool calls
92	
93	        Returns:
94	            True if the text contains a tool call, False otherwise
95	        """
96	        if not self.tools:
97	            return False
98	        return self.detector.has_tool_call(text)
99	
100	    def parse_non_stream(self, full_text: str) -> Tuple[str, list[ToolCallItem]]:
101	        """
102	        One-time parsing of the full text to extract tool calls.
103	
104	        Args:
105	            full_text: The complete text to parse
106	
107	        Returns:
108	            A tuple containing:
109	            - The remaining text after parsing that was not consumed by the detector (can be treated as normal text)
110	            - A list of tool calls parsed from the text
111	        """
112	        if not self.tools:
113	            return full_text, []
114	        parsed_result = self.detector.detect_and_parse(full_text, self.tools)
115	        tool_call_list = parsed_result.calls
116	        if tool_call_list:
117	            return parsed_result.normal_text, tool_call_list
118	        else:
119	            return full_text, []
120	
121	    def parse_stream_chunk(self, chunk_text: str) -> Tuple[str, list[ToolCallItem]]:
122	        """
123	        Streaming incremental parsing of chunks of text as they arrive.
124	
125	        Args:
126	            chunk_text: The new chunk of text to parse
127	
128	        Returns:
129	            A tuple containing:
130	            - The normal text that should be displayed to the user
131	            - A list of tool calls parsed from the chunk
132	        """
133	        if not self.tools:
134	            return chunk_text, []
135	        final_normal_text = ""
136	        final_calls = []
137	
138	        sp_result = self.detector.parse_streaming_increment(chunk_text, self.tools)
139	        if sp_result.normal_text:
140	            final_normal_text = sp_result.normal_text
141	        if sp_result.calls:
142	            final_calls.extend(sp_result.calls)
143	            final_normal_text = sp_result.normal_text
144	
145	        return final_normal_text, final_calls
146	
147	    def get_structure_tag(self) -> LegacyStructuralTagResponseFormat:
148	        """
149	        Generate a structural tag response format for all available tools.
150	
151	        This creates the necessary structural tags that guide the model's output format.
152	        """
153	        tool_structures: List[StructuresResponseFormat] = list()
154	        tool_trigger_set: Set[str] = set()
155	
156	        get_structure_info = self.detector.structure_info()
157	        for tool in self.tools:
158	            function = tool.function
159	            name = function.name
160	            assert name is not None
161	            info = get_structure_info(name)
162	
163	            # accept all if not strict, otherwise only accept the schema
164	            is_strict = (
165	                function.strict or self.tool_strict_level >= ToolStrictLevel.PARAMETER
166	            )
167	            schema = function.parameters if is_strict else {}
168	
169	            tool_structures.append(
170	                StructuresResponseFormat(
171	                    begin=info.begin,
172	                    schema=schema or {},  # type: ignore
173	                    end=info.end,
174	                )
175	            )
176	            tool_trigger_set.add(info.trigger)
177	
178	        # TODO(dark): move this into new structural tag format
179	        # This requires all grammar backend support the new format
180	        return LegacyStructuralTagResponseFormat(
181	            type="structural_tag",
182	            structures=tool_structures,
183	            triggers=list(tool_trigger_set),
184	        )
185	
186	    def get_structure_constraint(
187	        self, tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["auto", "required"]]
188	    ) -> Optional[ToolCallConstraint]:
189	        """
190	        Returns the appropriate structure constraint for tool calls based on the tool_choice.
191	        The constraint is used to guide the model's output format.
192	
193	        Args:
194	            tool_choice: The tool choice setting from the request
195	
196	        Returns:
197	            A tuple of (constraint_type, constraint_value) to be added to sampling parameters,
198	            or None if no constraint applies.
199	        """
200	        # NOTE: structural_tag only supports JSON-compatible content between the begin and end.
201	        # It cannot parse or validate function call Pythonic or XML-ish syntax.
202	        if (
203	            self.detector.supports_structural_tag()
204	            and tool_choice == "auto"
205	            and (
206	                any(tool.function.strict for tool in self.tools)
207	                or self.tool_strict_level >= ToolStrictLevel.FUNCTION
208	            )
209	        ):
210	            tag = self.get_structure_tag()
211	            return ("structural_tag", tag)
212	        elif tool_choice == "required" or isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
213	            json_schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, tool_choice)
214	            return ("json_schema", json_schema)
215

12	def _find_common_prefix(s1: str, s2: str) -> str:
13	    prefix = ""
14	    min_length = min(len(s1), len(s2))
15	    for i in range(0, min_length):
16	        if s1[i] == s2[i]:
17	            prefix += s1[i]
18	        else:
19	            break
20	    return prefix
21	
22	
23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise
50	
51	
52	def _is_complete_json(input_str: str) -> bool:
53	    try:
54	        orjson.loads(input_str)
55	        return True
56	    except JSONDecodeError:
57	        return False

60	def _get_tool_schema_defs(tools: List[Tool]) -> dict:
61	    """
62	    Get consolidated $defs from all tools, validating for conflicts.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found
72	    """
73	    all_defs = {}
74	    for tool in tools:
75	        if tool.function.parameters is None:
76	            continue
77	        defs = tool.function.parameters.get("$defs", {})
78	        for def_name, def_schema in defs.items():
79	            if def_name in all_defs and all_defs[def_name] != def_schema:
80	                raise ValueError(
81	                    f"Tool definition '{def_name}' has "
82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs
88	
89	
90	def _get_tool_schema(tool: Tool) -> dict:
91	    return {
92	        "properties": {
93	            "name": {"type": "string", "enum": [tool.function.name]},
94	            "parameters": (
95	                tool.function.parameters
96	                if tool.function.parameters
97	                else {"type": "object", "properties": {}}
98	            ),
99	        },
100	        "required": ["name", "parameters"],
101	    }
102	
103	
104	def infer_type_from_json_schema(schema: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
105	    """
106	    Infer the primary type of a parameter from JSON Schema.
107	
108	    Supports complex JSON Schema structures including:
109	    - Direct type field (including type arrays)
110	    - anyOf/oneOf: parameter can be any of multiple types
111	    - enum: parameter must be one of enum values
112	    - allOf: parameter must satisfy all type definitions
113	    - properties: inferred as object type
114	    - items: inferred as array type
115	
116	    Args:
117	        schema: JSON Schema definition
118	
119	    Returns:
120	        Inferred type ('string', 'number', 'object', 'array', etc.) or None
121	    """
122	    if not isinstance(schema, dict):
123	        return None
124	
125	    # Priority 1: Direct type field (including type arrays)
126	    if "type" in schema:
127	        type_value = schema["type"]
128	        if isinstance(type_value, str):
129	            return type_value
130	        elif isinstance(type_value, list) and type_value:
131	            # Handle type arrays: return first non-null type
132	            non_null_types = [t for t in type_value if t != "null"]
133	            if non_null_types:
134	                return non_null_types[0]
135	            return "string"  # If only null, default to string
136	
137	    # Priority 2: Handle anyOf/oneOf
138	    if "anyOf" in schema or "oneOf" in schema:
139	        schemas = schema.get("anyOf") or schema.get("oneOf")
140	        types = []
141	
142	        if isinstance(schemas, list):
143	            for sub_schema in schemas:
144	                inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
145	                if inferred_type:
146	                    types.append(inferred_type)
147	
148	            if types:
149	                # If all types are the same, return unified type
150	                if len(set(types)) == 1:
151	                    return types[0]
152	                # When types differ, prioritize string (safest)
153	                if "string" in types:
154	                    return "string"
155	                # Otherwise return first type
156	                return types[0]
157	
158	    # Priority 3: Handle enum (infer type from enum values)
159	    if "enum" in schema and isinstance(schema["enum"], list):
160	        if not schema["enum"]:
161	            return "string"
162	
163	        # Infer type from enum values
164	        enum_types = set()
165	        for value in schema["enum"]:
166	            if value is None:
167	                enum_types.add("null")
168	            elif isinstance(value, bool):
169	                enum_types.add("boolean")
170	            elif isinstance(value, int):
171	                enum_types.add("integer")
172	            elif isinstance(value, float):
173	                enum_types.add("number")
174	            elif isinstance(value, str):
175	                enum_types.add("string")
176	            elif isinstance(value, list):
177	                enum_types.add("array")
178	            elif isinstance(value, dict):
179	                enum_types.add("object")
180	
181	        # If type is uniform, return that type
182	        if len(enum_types) == 1:
183	            return enum_types.pop()
184	        # Mixed types, prioritize string
185	        return "string"
186	
187	    # Priority 4: Handle allOf (must satisfy all types)
188	    if "allOf" in schema and isinstance(schema["allOf"], list):
189	        schemas = schema["allOf"]
190	        for sub_schema in schemas:
191	            inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
192	            if inferred_type and inferred_type != "string":
193	                return inferred_type
194	        return "string"
195	
196	    # Priority 5: Infer object type
197	    if "properties" in schema:
198	        return "object"
199	
200	    # Priority 6: Infer array type
201	    if "items" in schema:
202	        return "array"
203	
204	    return None
205	
206	
207	def get_json_schema_constraint(
208	    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
209	) -> Optional[dict]:
210	    """
211	    Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        tool_choice: The tool choice specification
215	
216	    Returns:
217	        JSON schema dict, or None if no valid tools found
218	    """
219	
220	    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
221	        # For specific function choice, return the user's parameters schema directly
222	        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
223	        for tool in tools:
224	            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
225	                return {
226	                    "type": "array",
227	                    "minItems": 1,
228	                    "maxItems": 1,
229	                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
230	                }
231	        return None
232	    elif tool_choice == "required":
233	        json_schema = {
234	            "type": "array",
235	            "minItems": 1,
236	            "items": {
237	                "type": "object",
238	                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
239	            },
240	        }
241	        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
242	        if json_schema_defs:
243	            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
244	        return json_schema
245	
246	    return None
247

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []

224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,

239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )

305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)

310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program

313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend

316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state

6

8	
9	from pydantic import BaseModel
10	
11	import sglang as sgl
12	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import build_regex_from_object
13	
14	character_regex = (
15	    r"""\{\n"""
16	    + r"""    "name": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
17	    + r"""    "house": "(Gryffindor|Slytherin|Ravenclaw|Hufflepuff)",\n"""
18	    + r"""    "blood status": "(Pure-blood|Half-blood|Muggle-born)",\n"""
19	    + r"""    "occupation": "(student|teacher|auror|ministry of magic|death eater|order of the phoenix)",\n"""
20	    + r"""    "wand": \{\n"""
21	    + r"""        "wood": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
22	    + r"""        "core": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
23	    + r"""        "length": [0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{0,2}\n"""
24	    + r"""    \},\n"""
25	    + r"""    "alive": "(Alive|Deceased)",\n"""
26	    + r"""    "patronus": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
27	    + r"""    "bogart": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}"\n"""
28	    + r"""\}"""
29	)
30	
31	
32	@sgl.function
33	def character_gen(s, name):
34	    s += (
35	        name
36	        + " is a character in Harry Potter. Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
37	    )
38	    s += "The constrained regex is:\n"
39	    s += character_regex + "\n"
40	    s += "The JSON output is:\n"
41	    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)
42	
43	
44	def driver_character_gen():
45	    state = character_gen.run(name="Hermione Granger")
46	    print(state.text())
47	
48	
49	class Weapon(str, Enum):
50	    sword = "sword"
51	    axe = "axe"
52	    mace = "mace"
53	    spear = "spear"
54	    bow = "bow"
55	    crossbow = "crossbow"
56	
57	
58	class Wizard(BaseModel):
59	    name: str
60	    age: int
61	    weapon: Weapon
62	
63	
64	@sgl.function
65	def pydantic_wizard_gen(s):
66	    s += "Give me a description about a wizard in the JSON format.\n"
67	    s += sgl.gen(
68	        "character",
69	        max_tokens=128,
70	        temperature=0,
71	        regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard),  # Requires pydantic >= 2.0
72	    )
73	
74	
75	def driver_pydantic_wizard_gen():
76	    state = pydantic_wizard_gen.run()
77	    print(state.text())
78	
79	
80	if __name__ == "__main__":
81	    sgl.set_default_backend(sgl.RuntimeEndpoint("http://localhost:30000"))
82	    driver_character_gen()

1	import sglang as sgl
2	
3	character_regex = (
4	    r"""\{\n"""
5	    + r"""    "姓名": "[^"]{1,32}",\n"""
6	    + r"""    "学院": "(格兰芬多|赫奇帕奇|拉文克劳|斯莱特林)",\n"""
7	    + r"""    "血型": "(纯血|混血|麻瓜)",\n"""
8	    + r"""    "职业": "(学生|教师|傲罗|魔法部|食死徒|凤凰社成员)",\n"""
9	    + r"""    "魔杖": \{\n"""
10	    + r"""        "材质": "[^"]{1,32}",\n"""
11	    + r"""        "杖芯": "[^"]{1,32}",\n"""
12	    + r"""        "长度": [0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{0,2}\n"""
13	    + r"""    \},\n"""
14	    + r"""    "存活": "(存活|死亡)",\n"""
15	    + r"""    "守护神": "[^"]{1,32}",\n"""
16	    + r"""    "博格特": "[^"]{1,32}"\n"""
17	    + r"""\}"""
18	)
19	
20	
21	@sgl.function
22	def character_gen(s, name):
23	    s += name + " 是一名哈利波特系列小说中的角色。请填写以下关于这个角色的信息。"
24	    s += """\
25	这是一个例子
26	{
27	    "姓名": "哈利波特",
28	    "学院": "格兰芬多",
29	    "血型": "混血",
30	    "职业": "学生",
31	    "魔杖": {
32	        "材质": "冬青木",
33	        "杖芯": "凤凰尾羽",
34	        "长度": 11.0
35	    },
36	    "存活": "存活",
37	    "守护神": "麋鹿",
38	    "博格特": "摄魂怪"
39	}
40	"""
41	    s += f"现在请你填写{name}的信息：\n"
42	    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)
43	
44	
45	def main():
46	    backend = sgl.RuntimeEndpoint("http://localhost:30000")
47	    sgl.set_default_backend(backend)
48	    ret = character_gen.run(name="赫敏格兰杰", temperature=0)
49	    print(ret.text())
50	
51	
52	if __name__ == "__main__":
53	    main()

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )

223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )

304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state

92	
93	def run_program_batch(
94	    program,
95	    backend,
96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))
113	
114	    if generator_style:
115	        return _run_program_batch_generator(
116	            program,
117	            backend,
118	            batch_arguments,
119	            default_sampling_para,
120	            num_threads,
121	            progress_bar,
122	        )
123	
124	    # Original code path when generator_style=False
125	    if num_threads == 1:
126	        rets = []
127	        if progress_bar:
128	            for arguments in tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments):
129	                rets.append(
130	                    run_program(
131	                        program,
132	                        backend,
133	                        (),
134	                        arguments,
135	                        default_sampling_para,
136	                        False,
137	                        True,
138	                    )
139	                )
140	        else:
141	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
142	                rets.append(
143	                    run_program(
144	                        program,
145	                        backend,
146	                        (),
147	                        arguments,
148	                        default_sampling_para,
149	                        False,
150	                        True,
151	                    )
152	                )
153	    else:
154	        if progress_bar:
155	            pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments))
156	
157	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
158	            futures = []
159	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
160	                futures.append(
161	                    executor.submit(
162	                        run_program,
163	                        program,
164	                        backend,
165	                        (),
166	                        arguments,
167	                        default_sampling_para,
168	                        False,
169	                        True,
170	                    )
171	                )
172	                if progress_bar:
173	                    futures[-1].add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
174	
175	            rets = [f.result() for f in futures]
176	        rets[-1].sync()
177	
178	        if progress_bar:
179	            pbar.close()
180	
181	    return rets
182	
183	
184	def _run_program_batch_generator(
185	    program,
186	    backend,
187	    batch_arguments,
188	    default_sampling_para,
189	    num_threads,
190	    progress_bar,
191	):
192	    """Helper function that yields results one by one using chunking to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor."""
193	    if num_threads == 1:
194	        iterator = tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments) if progress_bar else batch_arguments
195	        for arguments in iterator:
196	            yield run_program(
197	                program,
198	                backend,
199	                (),
200	                arguments,
201	                default_sampling_para,
202	                False,
203	                True,
204	            )
205	    else:
206	        pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments)) if progress_bar else None
207	
208	        # Process in chunks to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor
209	        # Otherwise, ThreadPoolExecutor.submit will block after adding certain number of tasks
210	        # so we will never reach "yield" until all tasks are done
211	        chunk_size = 200
212	
213	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
214	            for chunk_start in range(0, len(batch_arguments), chunk_size):
215	                chunk_end = min(chunk_start + chunk_size, len(batch_arguments))
216	                chunk_futures = []
217	
218	                # Submit chunk of tasks
219	                for i in range(chunk_start, chunk_end):
220	                    future = executor.submit(
221	                        run_program,
222	                        program,
223	                        backend,
224	                        (),
225	                        batch_arguments[i],
226	                        default_sampling_para,
227	                        False,
228	                        True,
229	                    )
230	                    if pbar:
231	                        future.add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
232	                    chunk_futures.append(future)
233	
234	                # Yield results from this chunk as they complete
235	                for future in chunk_futures:
236	                    yield future.result()
237	
238	        if pbar:
239	            pbar.close()
240	
241	
242	def cache_program(program, backend):
243	    from sglang.lang.tracer import extract_prefix_by_tracing
244	
245	    prefix = extract_prefix_by_tracing(program, backend)
246	    if prefix and len(prefix) > 64:
247	        backend.cache_prefix(prefix)
248	
249	
250	class StreamExecutor:
251	    """A stream executor that executes SGL expressions in a background thread."""
252	
253	    def __init__(
254	        self,
255	        backend,
256	        arguments,
257	        default_sampling_para,
258	        chat_template,
259	        stream,
260	        num_api_spec_tokens=None,
261	        use_thread=True,
262	    ):
263	        from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
264	
265	        self.sid = uuid.uuid4().hex
266	        self.backend: BaseBackend = backend
267	        self.arguments: Dict[str, Any] = arguments
268	        self.default_sampling_para = default_sampling_para
269	        self.stream = stream
270	
271	        self.variables = {}  # Dict[name: str -> value: str]
272	        self.variable_event = {}  # Dict[name: str -> event: threading.Event]
273	        self.meta_info = {}  # Dict[name: str -> info: str]
274	        self.is_finished = False
275	        self.error_ = None
276	
277	        # For completion
278	        self.text_ = ""  # The full text
279	
280	        # For chat
281	        self.messages_ = []  # The messages in the OpenAI API format
282	        self.chat_template = chat_template or self.backend.get_chat_template()
283	        self.cur_role = None
284	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = None
285	
286	        # For vision
287	        self.images_ = []
288	        self.cur_images = []
289	
290	        # For fork/join
291	        self.fork_start_text_pos = None
292	
293	        # For speculative execution
294	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
295	        self.speculated_text = ""
296	
297	        # Worker thread
298	        self.use_thread = use_thread
299	        if self.use_thread:
300	            self.queue = queue.Queue()
301	
302	            def _run_worker_in_context():
303	                self._thread_worker_func()
304	
305	            self.worker = threading.Thread(
306	                target=contextvars.copy_context().run, args=(_run_worker_in_context,)
307	            )
308	            self.worker.start()
309	
310	        # For streaming
311	        if stream:
312	            self.stream_text_event = threading.Event()
313	            self.stream_var_event = {}
314	        else:
315	            self.stream_text_event = None
316	            self.stream_var_event = None
317	
318	    def submit(self, expr: SglExpr):
319	        self._init_var_event(expr)
320	
321	        if self.use_thread:
322	            self.queue.put(expr)
323	        else:
324	            self._execute(expr)
325	
326	    def sync(self):
327	        if self.use_thread:
328	            self.queue.join()
329	
330	    def get_var(self, name):
331	        if name in self.variable_event:
332	            self.variable_event[name].wait()
333	        return self.variables[name]
334	
335	    def set_var(self, name, value):
336	        self.variables[name] = value
337	
338	    def get_meta_info(self, name, timeout=None):
339	        if name in self.variable_event:
340	            got = self.variable_event[name].wait(timeout)
341	            if not got:
342	                raise TimeoutError(f"Timeout while waiting for event '{name}'")
343	        ret = self.meta_info.get(name, None)
344	        return ret
345	
346	    def fork(
347	        self,
348	        size: int = 1,
349	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
350	    ):
351	        if size > 1 and str(self.text_):
352	            self.submit(SglCommitLazy())
353	
354	        self.sync()
355	        size = int(size)
356	
357	        exes = [
358	            StreamExecutor(
359	                self.backend,
360	                self.arguments,
361	                self.default_sampling_para,
362	                self.chat_template,
363	                self.stream,
364	            )
365	            for _ in range(size)
366	        ]
367	        for i in range(size):
368	            exes[i].variables = dict(self.variables)
369	            exes[i].text_ = str(self.text_)
370	            exes[i].messages_ = list(self.messages_)
371	            exes[i].cur_role = self.cur_role
372	            exes[i].cur_role_begin_pos = self.cur_role_begin_pos
373	            exes[i].fork_start_text_pos = len(self.text_)
374	            exes[i].images_ = list(self.images_)
375	
376	            # TODO(ying): handle API speculative execution
377	
378	        return exes
379	
380	    def text(self):
381	        self.sync()
382	        return self.text_
383	
384	    def messages(self):
385	        self.sync()
386	        return self.messages_
387	
388	    def error(self):
389	        self.sync()
390	        return self.error_
391	
392	    def end(self):
393	        if self.use_thread:
394	            if self.worker.is_alive():
395	                self.queue.put(None)
396	        self.backend.end_program(self)
397	
398	    def _thread_worker_func(self):
399	        error = None
400	
401	        while True:
402	            expr = self.queue.get()
403	            if expr is None:
404	                self.queue.task_done()
405	                break
406	
407	            try:
408	                self._execute(expr)
409	            except Exception as e:
410	                warnings.warn(f"Error in stream_executor: {get_exception_traceback()}")
411	                error = e
412	                break
413	            self.queue.task_done()
414	            if self.stream_text_event:
415	                self.stream_text_event.set()
416	
417	        # Clean the queue and events
418	        if error is not None:
419	            try:
420	                while True:
421	                    self.queue.task_done()
422	                    self.queue.get_nowait()
423	            except queue.Empty:
424	                pass
425	            for name in self.variable_event:
426	                self.variable_event[name].set()
427	            if self.stream_var_event:
428	                for name in self.stream_var_event:
429	                    self.stream_var_event[name].set()
430	            self.error_ = error
431	
432	        if self.stream_text_event:
433	            self.stream_text_event.set()
434	
435	        self.is_finished = True
436	
437	    def _execute(self, other):
438	        if isinstance(other, str):
439	            other = SglConstantText(other)
440	
441	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
442	
443	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
444	            self._execute_fill(other.value)
445	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
446	            self._execute_gen(other)
447	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
448	            self._execute_select(other)
449	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
450	            for x in other.expr_list:
451	                self._execute(x)
452	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
453	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
454	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
455	            self._execute_role_end(other)
456	        elif isinstance(other, SglImage):
457	            self._execute_image(other)
458	        elif isinstance(other, SglVideo):
459	            self._execute_video(other)
460	        elif isinstance(other, SglVariable):
461	            self._execute_variable(other)
462	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
463	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
464	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
465	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
466	        elif isinstance(other, SglCommitLazy):
467	            self._execute_commit_lazy_operations(other)
468	        elif isinstance(other, SglConcateAndAppend):
469	            if (
470	                global_config.enable_parallel_encoding
471	                and self.backend.support_concate_and_append
472	            ):
473	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(other)
474	            else:
475	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_text(other)
476	        elif isinstance(other, SglSeparateReasoning):
477	            self._execute_separate_reasoning(other)
478	        else:
479	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type: {type(other)}")
480	
481	    def _execute_fill(self, value: str, prefix=False):
482	        value = str(value)
483	
484	        if (
485	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
486	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
487	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
488	            and not prefix
489	        ):
490	            self.backend.spec_fill(value)
491	            return
492	
493	        if self.speculated_text.startswith(value):
494	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[len(value) :]
495	        else:
496	            self.speculated_text = ""
497	
498	        self.text_ += value
499	
500	    def _execute_image(self, expr: SglImage):
501	        path = expr.path
502	
503	        base64_data = encode_image_base64(path)
504	
505	        self.images_.append((path, base64_data))
506	        self.cur_images.append((path, base64_data))
507	        self.text_ += self.chat_template.image_token
508	
509	    def _execute_video(self, expr: SglVideo):
510	        path = expr.path
511	        num_frames = expr.num_frames
512	
513	        base64_data = encode_video_base64(path, num_frames)
514	
515	        self.images_.append((path, base64_data))
516	        self.cur_images.append((path, base64_data))
517	        self.text_ += self.chat_template.image_token
518	
519	    def _spec_gen(self, sampling_params):
520	        stop = sampling_params.stop
521	        max_new_tokens = sampling_params.max_new_tokens
522	        meta_info = {}
523	
524	        def regen():
525	            nonlocal meta_info
526	
527	            sampling_params.max_new_tokens = max(
528	                sampling_params.max_new_tokens, self.num_api_spec_tokens
529	            )
530	            sampling_params.stop = None
531	            self.speculated_text, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
532	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
533	            )
534	
535	        def find_stop():
536	            if isinstance(stop, str):
537	                return self.speculated_text.find(stop)
538	            elif isinstance(stop, (tuple, list)):
539	                pos = -1
540	                for stop_str in stop:
541	                    stop_pos = self.speculated_text.find(stop_str)
542	                    if stop_pos != -1 and (pos == -1 or stop_pos < pos):
543	                        pos = stop_pos
544	                return pos
545	            else:
546	                raise Exception("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
547	
548	        if stop is None:
549	            if len(self.speculated_text) < max_new_tokens:
550	                regen()
551	            comp = self.speculated_text[:max_new_tokens]
552	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[max_new_tokens:]
553	        elif isinstance(stop, (str, list, tuple)):
554	            if self.speculated_text == "":
555	                regen()
556	            stop_pos = find_stop()
557	            if stop_pos == -1:
558	                stop_pos = min(
559	                    sampling_params.max_new_tokens,
560	                    len(self.speculated_text),
561	                )
562	            comp = self.speculated_text[:stop_pos]
563	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[stop_pos:]
564	        else:
565	            raise ValueError("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
566	
567	        return comp, meta_info
568

775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",
798	            "top_k",
799	            "min_p",
800	            "frequency_penalty",
801	            "presence_penalty",
802	            "ignore_eos",
803	            "return_logprob",
804	            "logprob_start_len",
805	            "top_logprobs_num",
806	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
807	            "dtype",
808	            "regex",
809	            "json_schema",
810	        ]:
811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
812	            if value is not None:
813	                setattr(clone, item, value)
814	
815	        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
816	            if clone.stop == ():
817	                clone.stop = []
818	            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
819	                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
820	            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str
821	
822	        return clone

184	def _run_program_batch_generator(
185	    program,
186	    backend,
187	    batch_arguments,
188	    default_sampling_para,
189	    num_threads,
190	    progress_bar,
191	):
192	    """Helper function that yields results one by one using chunking to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor."""
193	    if num_threads == 1:
194	        iterator = tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments) if progress_bar else batch_arguments
195	        for arguments in iterator:
196	            yield run_program(
197	                program,
198	                backend,
199	                (),
200	                arguments,
201	                default_sampling_para,
202	                False,
203	                True,
204	            )
205	    else:
206	        pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments)) if progress_bar else None
207	
208	        # Process in chunks to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor
209	        # Otherwise, ThreadPoolExecutor.submit will block after adding certain number of tasks
210	        # so we will never reach "yield" until all tasks are done
211	        chunk_size = 200
212	
213	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
214	            for chunk_start in range(0, len(batch_arguments), chunk_size):
215	                chunk_end = min(chunk_start + chunk_size, len(batch_arguments))
216	                chunk_futures = []
217	
218	                # Submit chunk of tasks
219	                for i in range(chunk_start, chunk_end):
220	                    future = executor.submit(
221	                        run_program,
222	                        program,
223	                        backend,
224	                        (),
225	                        batch_arguments[i],
226	                        default_sampling_para,
227	                        False,
228	                        True,
229	                    )
230	                    if pbar:
231	                        future.add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
232	                    chunk_futures.append(future)
233	
234	                # Yield results from this chunk as they complete
235	                for future in chunk_futures:
236	                    yield future.result()
237	
238	        if pbar:
239	            pbar.close()

373	            exes[i].fork_start_text_pos = len(self.text_)
374	            exes[i].images_ = list(self.images_)
375	
376	            # TODO(ying): handle API speculative execution
377	
378	        return exes
379	
380	    def text(self):
381	        self.sync()
382	        return self.text_
383	
384	    def messages(self):
385	        self.sync()
386	        return self.messages_
387	
388	    def error(self):
389	        self.sync()
390	        return self.error_
391	
392	    def end(self):
393	        if self.use_thread:
394	            if self.worker.is_alive():
395	                self.queue.put(None)
396	        self.backend.end_program(self)
397	
398	    def _thread_worker_func(self):
399	        error = None
400	
401	        while True:
402	            expr = self.queue.get()
403	            if expr is None:
404	                self.queue.task_done()
405	                break
406	
407	            try:
408	                self._execute(expr)
409	            except Exception as e:
410	                warnings.warn(f"Error in stream_executor: {get_exception_traceback()}")
411	                error = e
412	                break
413	            self.queue.task_done()
414	            if self.stream_text_event:
415	                self.stream_text_event.set()
416	
417	        # Clean the queue and events
418	        if error is not None:
419	            try:
420	                while True:
421	                    self.queue.task_done()
422	                    self.queue.get_nowait()
423	            except queue.Empty:
424	                pass
425	            for name in self.variable_event:
426	                self.variable_event[name].set()
427	            if self.stream_var_event:
428	                for name in self.stream_var_event:
429	                    self.stream_var_event[name].set()
430	            self.error_ = error
431	
432	        if self.stream_text_event:
433	            self.stream_text_event.set()
434	
435	        self.is_finished = True
436	
437	    def _execute(self, other):
438	        if isinstance(other, str):
439	            other = SglConstantText(other)
440	
441	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
442	
443	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
444	            self._execute_fill(other.value)
445	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
446	            self._execute_gen(other)
447	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
448	            self._execute_select(other)
449	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
450	            for x in other.expr_list:
451	                self._execute(x)
452	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
453	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
454	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
455	            self._execute_role_end(other)
456	        elif isinstance(other, SglImage):
457	            self._execute_image(other)
458	        elif isinstance(other, SglVideo):
459	            self._execute_video(other)
460	        elif isinstance(other, SglVariable):
461	            self._execute_variable(other)
462	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
463	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
464	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
465	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
466	        elif isinstance(other, SglCommitLazy):
467	            self._execute_commit_lazy_operations(other)
468	        elif isinstance(other, SglConcateAndAppend):
469	            if (
470	                global_config.enable_parallel_encoding
471	                and self.backend.support_concate_and_append
472	            ):
473	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(other)
474	            else:
475	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_text(other)
476	        elif isinstance(other, SglSeparateReasoning):
477	            self._execute_separate_reasoning(other)
478	        else:
479	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type: {type(other)}")
480	
481	    def _execute_fill(self, value: str, prefix=False):
482	        value = str(value)
483	
484	        if (
485	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
486	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
487	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
488	            and not prefix
489	        ):
490	            self.backend.spec_fill(value)
491	            return
492	
493	        if self.speculated_text.startswith(value):
494	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[len(value) :]
495	        else:
496	            self.speculated_text = ""
497	
498	        self.text_ += value
499

23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel

26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
